## PERFORMANCE OPPORTUNITIES

Make your meta description eloquent and appealing, neither too short nor too long.

The meta description appears in search result pages.

Ideally, it should be between 50 and 160 characters.

It has a relatively big impact on how the website ranks in search engines. It's also a key factor people use to choose your page over competition to get what they are looking for.

Fill the missing alt attributes on your images.

Alt text is the written copy that appears in place of an image on a web page if the image fails to load on a user's screen.

This text helps screen-reading tools describe images to visually impaired readers and allows search engines to better crawl and rank your website.

hr.com has 50 images with a missing alt attribute.

results.text:

## Founded Year

1999

## Stage

Series B | Alive

## Total Raised

$2.88M

## Last Raised

$1.5M | 25 yrs ago

## Mosaic Score The Mosaic Score is an algorithm that measures the overall financial health and market potential of private companies.

+16 points in the past 30 days

## About HR.com

HR.com is a resource and networking platform focused on human resources education and certification within the HR industry. The company provides HR certification preparation, legal compliance guidance, industry research, and educational webcasts and events. HR.com serves HR professionals seeking education and recertification credits. It was founded in 1999 and is based in Tampa, Florida.

## Headquarters Location

4890 West Kennedy Boulevard Suite 500

Tampa, Florida, 33609,

United States

877-222-2222

An all inclusive learning and certification program for all roles within HR

## Latest HR.com News

New Study Identifies Critical Gaps and Future-Proofing Strategies for Talent Acquisition in 2026

Feb 8, 2026

IRVINE, CA / ACCESS Newswire / February 4, 2026 / While 77% of human resource (HR) professionals identify talent acquisition (TA) as a top five organizational priority, more than half are struggling with outdated or insufficient processes, according to a comprehensive new analysis by The HR Research Institute (HRRI), the research arm of HR.com. The study, Hiring Trends and Strategies 2026: Future-Proof Your Talent Acquisition Process conducted in partnership with Accurate , the world's largest independent provider of compliant background screening and monitoring solutions, also found that 58% of organizations have “subpar” recruitment functions, categorized as nonexistent, chaotic, or basic/reactive. These shortcomings carry a heavy financial burden. A single bad hire can cost an organization roughly 30% of that employee's annual earnings. The impact of these “regrettable hires” is widespread: 43% of HR professionals report they would rehire fewer than half of the candidates they brought on last year. “We're seeing more organizations recognize talent acquisition as a strategic priority,” said Tim Dowd, CEO of Accurate. “But this research shows that real impact only happens when that priority is backed

Home Talent Acquisition HR.com Acquires Exclusive HR Rights to Advos (HRMarketer) Strategic Marketing Software

- Talent Acquisition

Facebook

Twitter

Linkedin

HR.com has acquired the exclusive HR rights to advos strategic marketing software, and has incorporated the technology into a new marketing platform for the HR Marketplace, HR Market Smarts.

Advos, known as HRmarketer until earlier this year, will work with HR.com to further develop Market Smarts®. The platform helps HR solution providers make smart, informed marketing choices that enable them to build the brand visibility and credibility that they need to accelerate business growth. Market Smarts is bolstered by integration with HR.com’s research capabilities and software tools.

“Every marketing team promoting an HR solution or service needs Market Smarts,” HR.com CEO Debbie McGrath said. “Marketers who embed Market Smarts as a part of their everyday workflow will be more informed, discover better marketing opportunities, and have a major strategic advantage over their competitors trying to reach HR buyers and other key audiences.”

“This partnership is a win-win for HR.com and advos,” said advos CEO Mark Willaman.

results.title: HR.com and Next Concept HR Association (NCHRA) Combine to Expand HR Offerings
results.author: HR.com
results.text:
HR.com and Next Concept HR Association (NCHRA) Combine to Expand HR Offerings Accessibility Statement Skip Navigation

SAN FRANCISCO and JACKSON'S POINT, Ontario, Oct. 15, 2019 /PRNewswire-PRWeb/ -- HR.com and Next Concept HR Association (NCHRA) have combined to form a powerful membership-based organization centered around creating value and career development opportunities for HR professionals.

This partnership will extend resources, education, career development, networking and compliance content in HR to 1.75 million HR professionals adding a specialized focus for members in California. Collaboratively, HR.com and NCHRA will continue to provide HR events including the HR West Conference, the HR TechXpo conference, and regional HR meetups to grow attendance and offer broader content and sponsorship opportunities.

"This is a really great move for all HR professionals involved and will accelerate HR.com's mission of maximizing human potential and reaching HR professionals everywhere," said Debbie McGrath, CEO of HR.com. "We look forward to continually advancing the offerings, resources, and learnings available for our HR.com community and members, as we serve as the go-to source for all HR learning and development." Greg Morton, NCHRA CEO added, "recognizing HR.com's alignment with our mission and values, our membership overwhelmingly voiced support of this transaction further recognizing the added value it brings to them, the HR professionals and members."

According to HR.com's HR Research Institute, 82% of employees view HR as non-strategic to their organization. Collectively, HR.com and NCHRA will focus on helping HR professionals take their traditional administrative approach to become more strategic and provide more insightful value to their organization.

"The strategic function in the human capital industry is playing a bigger and bigger role to the point that HR needs to streamline their manual processes and improve their efficiencies with the use of innovative technologies," stated Debbie McGrath, CEO of HR.com. "This will give HR departments time and funds to focus on other more strategic roles."

By early 2020, NCHRA members and community members can expect to see their membership features expand significantly. HR.com is a leader in HR research powering the HR Research Institute which provides its members and HR community with the most relevant, applicable human capital insights available. The latest report, The DNA of Human Capital: Trends Report 2019, features twelve research-based "Traits", each relating to a key strategic HR issue.

About HR.com At HR.com, we are committed to creating inspired and informed workforces by maximizing human potential around the world. Over 1.75 million HR professionals rely on HR.com as the foremost, trusted industry resource for education, career development, networking and compliance. Offerings include the largest network of HR executives, leading-edge industry

results.author: HR.com Limited
results.text:
About Us: A Comprehensive HR Platform for Professionals Worldwide - HR.com
 
 
HR.com - Maximizing Human Potential
PrintForward
# WE ARE#HRDOTCOM
##### Our PurposeWe help create engaged and inspired workforces.
### Network with other HR Professionals.
Careers with Us

Embedded YouTube video
## Our values
* BHAG (Big Hairy Audacious Goal)
By 2040, we will impact 50% of the global workforce by empowering HR professionals in their careers, maximizing human potential.
* Mission
We provide personalized guidance and tools to help HR professionals maximize their potential, navigate their careers, and transform HR teams to engage and inspire their workforce.
* Vision
We see a world in which every HR professional has the education, skills, competencies, tools to be able to measure their impact in creating organizational value and inspired workforces.
## About HR.com
HR.com, the largest network of HR professionals, is committed to helping HR professionals advance and build meaningful careers and find the optimal solutions to enhance their job performance.
Genie giving feedback
Genie educating people
With products and resources rooted in education, research, and leveraging cutting-edge technology, we help at every career stage - and over 2 million HR pros agree! (How could that many people be wrong?)
By delivering best-in-class learning products, 500+ annual webcasts, 50+ world-class events, and innovative and thought-provoking research through the HR Research Institute, HR.com strives to inspire and strengthen workforces to change the world.
HR.com also offers the most comprehensive HR certification exam preparation and guarantees a passing score on all SHRM and HRCI certification exams. Technology and experience drive our customized solutions that will help you become the best and most successful version of yourself. Learn more at www.HR.com and maximize your potential.

results.title: JER HR Group Announces Acquisition of Human Resources Plus
results.author: JER HR Group
results.text:
JER HR Group Announces Acquisition of Human Resources Plus Accessibility Statement Skip Navigation

Rebecca Page, President Human Resources Plus, Inc.

JER HR Group LLC has acquired Human Resources Plus, Inc., a HR consulting firm and online HR document provider. JER HR Group is a national leader in compensation consulting with more than 30 years of experience in HR services. Human Resources Plus is a HR consulting firm and online HR document provider servicing small businesses primarily in Colorado, insurance companies and brokers, and multi-channel third party alliances.

NEW YORK, Jan. 19, 2023 /PRNewswire-PRWeb/ -- JER HR Group and Human Resources Plus jointly announce JER HR Group's acquisition of Human Resources Plus, Inc. in a private transaction. Human Resources Plus (HRP) is a HR consulting firm and online HR document provider servicing small businesses primarily in Colorado, insurance companies and brokers, and multi-channel third party alliances.

" JER HR Group is a recognized leader in compensation consulting and brings more than 30 years of experience in HR services, working with clients nationwide. Acquiring HRP, which has been in business since 2003, enhances JER HR Group's geographic footprint. The resources in the HRP online portal are part of the transaction and will be incorporated into DelegateHR, a centralized HR Document Management System scheduled to launch soon, and part of our Trainery brand of HCM solutions," said Mahesh Kumar, Managing Director of JER HR Group.

Human Resources Plus, Inc. is the third acquisition by JER HR Group within 12 months. "Our objective is to become a single source of HR information and services for our clients. We already have robust SaaS based HCM solutions under TraineryHCM and new product solutions in development which will launch this year. Integrating the consulting talent of HRP along with its online knowledge base expands our product capabilities and addresses our client needs," Kumar said.

Rebecca Page, Co-Founder and President of Human Resources Plus said, "Our focus has always been on our clients. The depth of experience, visionary growth, and performance of JER HR Group will provide expanded opportunities for our clients without losing the personal touch. Co-Founder Erin Osbourne and I, alongside our team, are excited to make the transition. JER HR Group is a perfect partner and an excellent choice to take us to the next level."

ABOUT JER HR Group is a leading compensation and HR consulting firm helping for-profit companies, nonprofit organizations, and government entities through the United States to recruit, develop, engage, and retain their greatest resource—People. JER HR Group is headquartered in New York City, New York with additional offices in Raleigh and Greensboro North Carolina, Fayetteville Arkansas, and Columbia South Carolina. JER HR Group is a certified minority owned business. For more

Home HR Core administration Payroll/Benefits Salary.com acquires CompXL

- Payroll/Benefits
- HR Core administration

Facebook

Twitter

Linkedin

Salary.com®, the market leader in total compensation management software and data solutions, announces the acquisition of HCR Software, maker of CompXL’s enterprise incentive compensation planning software. CompXL supports the management of merit raises, bonus, commissions, long term incentive awards, equity, and employee total rewards statements across an entire organization. With this acquisition, Salary.com builds on its core foundation of highly accurate and comprehensive data and software, completing the evolution of CompAnalyst® into the only Total Compensation Management Platform (“TCM”) that pairs Salary.com’s proprietary back-end technologies with a familiar spreadsheet format to make a flexible, scalable, enterprise-ready solution built for the cloud.

Kent Plunkett, CEO and co-founder of Salary.com says, “Most companies struggle to manage decision-making and communications around people’s pay and incentives. Our CompAnalyst Total Compensation Management Platform now offers enterprises everything they need to benchmark, manage, and communicate employee total rewards. Getting pay right
